title: cirsium setidens f. lanceolatum
intro: Cirsium setidens f. lanceolatum, a subspecies of the Cirsium setidens thistle plant, is a flowering plant native to North America. It is also known as the Woolly Thistle or Lanceleaf Woolly Thistle. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The leaves and flowers of Cirsium setidens f. lanceolatum can be prepared as a tea or tincture, or made into a salve or poultice for topical application, offering potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
